channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Delete record using JooqTemplate
item.description: 1. delete Delete records matching the given condition. // Single Condition public int delete(String table, Condition condition) public int delete(Table table, Condition condition) //...

item.title: WWDC 2026 - Xcode 27 Ships With Apple's Own Agent Skills: What They Are and How to Use Them
item.description: At WWDC 2026, Apple did something specific and easy to miss in the flood of Siri AI headlines: Xcode 27 now ships with a set of agent skills that Apple wrote itself. These are not a community...

item.title: Stop Fake Webhooks: Master HMAC Signatures in Laravel 🛡️
item.description: The Webhook Spoofing Vulnerability When engineering a B2B SaaS platform at Smart Tech Devs, relying on external services like Stripe, Shopify, or GitHub is inevitable. These services communicate...

item.title: I put my fleet of AI agents under a binary security veto — here's why
item.description: The most popular AI tools today give you power. Few give you a brake. And once you let an agent write code, touch data, or make a decision, the question that matters stops being "how smart is it?"...

item.title: Securing PostgreSQL, in the order an attacker would try things
item.description: Postgres has a well-earned reputation for security. Roles, privileges, host-based authentication, SCRAM. The primitives are all there, and they're good. And yet databases keep leaking. 82% of data...

item.title: Prompts to Production — A Full Stack Application by Sonnet with Catalyst
item.description: If you're anything like me, you think vibe coding is the most overhyped term in software development right now. Not because it doesn't work. But because most people doing it are using Claude like...

item.title: I am new to dev community I am building an on demand cab hailing service app I wanted someone to review it . Please Its being built with Fastify and react native. For contacting please take my discord lakshyasharma7762
I Built a Production RAG System on My M1 Mac for $0
item.description: I Built a Production RAG System on My M1 Mac for $0 Most RAG tutorials stop at "it answers questions." But answering questions is table stakes. The real question is: are the answers actually...

item.title: Xcode 27: The Future of Agent-Driven Development is Here
item.description: For the first time, Apple is bringing production-grade AI agents directly into the IDE. This isn't just code completion on steroids. Xcode 27 integrates coding agents from Anthropic, Google, and...

item.title: Your schema validation passes and the agent still picks the wrong tool. The bug is semantic.
item.description: Pydantic and JSON-schema guarantee the shape of a tool call. They say nothing about whether it was the right call for the user's intent. TL;DR: We put strict Pydantic validation on every tool call...

item.title: Google just fired a warning shot in the AI subscription price wars
item.description: Google just made it significantly cheaper to enjoy its budget AI subscription tier.

item.title: I tried Siri AI, and so far it actually works
item.description: Parents want one thing, and one thing only, out of AI: to add a list of soccer games or "spirit week" theme days from an email or a poorly formatted flyer onto their calendar in one shot. And I...

item.title: How Justin Ernest invested nearly $500M into hot startups without a traditional VC fund
item.description: Instead of spending a year raising a formal venture fund, the Sabertooth VC founder used a captive network of LPs to invest in startups like Anthropic, Anduril, and SpaceX.

item.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
item.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch...

item.title: Congress just gave DHS another $70 billion
item.description: Congress narrowly voted to fund President Donald Trump's mass deportation agenda, giving the Department of Homeland Security $70 billion over the next three years. The house voted 214 to 212 in...

item.title: The App Store is going to add subscription bundles soon
item.description: Later this year, you will be able to get bundled subscriptions for iPhone apps, as Apple announced it's expanding App Store bundles so they can include offers from different companies. It's...

item.title: GM thinks EVs can help offset AI’s energy suck with vehicle-to-grid tech
item.description: At an event in San Francisco today, General Motors made a series of announcements around EV batteries, energy storage, and grid resiliency in the face of growing electricity demand from AI data...

item.title: Locked in heated rivalry with researcher, Microsoft fixes 0-day they disclosed
item.description: A separate zero-day also disclosed by Nightmare Eclipse appears to be patched as well.

item.title: Hey, Siri, here’s what I actually want from AI
item.description: I'm desperate for a personal AI assistant, but do I really want to become the kind of person who can't function without the friendly robot voice in my phone?

item.title: Anthropic’s Fable 5 can make weirdly fun video games with the click of a button
item.description: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is going to be a big hit with the web's vibe coders.

item.title: Microsoft AI head calls out Anthropic for acting like Claude is conscious
item.description: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman says it's "really, really dangerous" for Anthropic to speculate about Claude's consciousness inside its "constitution," or the instructions that tell the model...

item.title: Can Voice Agents Handle Bilingual Customers? Benchmarking Frontier ASR on Code-Switched Speech
Nintendo is playing things too safe
item.description: Tuesday's Nintendo Direct showcase felt like an important moment for the company. With the Switch 2 heading into its second holiday season, one in which the hardware will be even more expensive...

item.title: Can tech companies learn to love cheaper AI models?
item.description: If those same AI workloads can be handled by cheaper models without affecting quality, it would mean a massive shift in the economics of AI.

item.title: WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence, and more
item.description: Apple primarily made the case for an improved experience with its long-standing Siri assistant, which like most other announcements had a hefty helping of AI.

item.title: Fitbit’s Charge 6 and Ace LTE are now as cheap as the new $100 Air
item.description: Whether you’re shopping for Father’s Day or trying to keep your kids entertained over summer break, you don’t need to spend a fortune to get a great Fitbit right now. You can currently pick up the...

item.title: Apple wants Europe to blink
item.description: It took a few years, but Apple finally made its AI look useful. Now millions of iPhone users in Europe are being told they won't be getting Siri AI anytime soon, if ever - and Apple wants them to...

item.title: Meta to Use Off-Site Business Data for Feed and AI Personalization
item.description: Meta on Tuesday announced that it will use information shared by other businesses to personalize users' feed and responses from its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot, expanding its scope beyond...

item.title: Anthropic Offers Mythos Upgrade for Cyber Partners and a ‘Safe’ Version for the Rest of You
item.description: Anthropic is releasing Claude Mythos 5 to trusted organizations and Claude Fable 5 to the public, a version it says can’t be used for cyberattacks.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today
item.description: Anthropic is releasing Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available to the public. The model comes with guardrails that block responses in high-risk areas like cybersecurity and biology.
